competitiveIntelligence.title: Multi-Agent AI Orchestration Competitive Analysis
competitiveIntelligence.purpose: Beta launch competitive positioning (AMC Beta, Mar 24 launch)
competitiveIntelligence.keyFinding: Multi-agent AI orchestration is emerging as a distinct product category in 2026, but most competitors are focused on vertical applications (coding, customer service, analytics) rather than general-purpose orchestration.
competitiveIntelligence.differentiationOpportunity: Faintech OS's strength is in cross-domain coordination with persistent memory — positioning should emphasize 'orchestration layer for your entire stack' rather than 'another AI agent for X use case.'

marketLandscape.categoryDefinition: Multi-Agent AI Orchestration: Systems that enable multiple AI agents to collaborate, share context, and coordinate workflows across different domains (not just single use case automation).

competitors.name: Anthropic (Claude)
competitors.keyGap: 50% concentration on software engineering - non-dev domains underserved

competitors.name: OpenAI (GPT-5)
competitors.keyGap: Multi-agent limited to coding workflows, session-based memory only

competitors.name: LangChain
competitors.keyGap: Framework not product, requires dev work to implement

recentSignals.title: Measuring AI Agent Autonomy in Practice
recentSignals.keyFindings: 99.9th percentile turn duration doubled from 25min to 45min in 3 months. Auto-approval rate increased from 20% → 40%+. Trust builds gradually. 50% of agent activity is software engineering.
recentSignals.implications: Beta onboarding should prioritize low-risk, high-clarity tasks first to build trust. Monitoring UX should be 'intervene when needed' not 'approve everything'. Self-uncertainty detection should be integrated.

recentSignals.title: GPT-5 Multi-Agent Mode Beta
recentSignals.keyFindings: Multi-agent mode limited to coding workflows. Session-based memory (clears between conversations, not persistent). No cross-domain coordination (agents stay within codebase context).
recentSignals.implications: Persistent memory is a competitive advantage. Cross-domain coordination is underserved. Flat-rate pricing could be attractive for SMBs who want predictable costs.

betaLaunchRecommendations.positioningStatement: Faintech OS is first general-purpose multi-agent orchestration layer with persistent memory — not just another coding agent.
pillars: Orchestrate Across Domains: Coordinate agents across retail operations, consulting projects, and marketing campaigns (not just code)
Memory That Persists: Business knowledge survives staff turnover and session breaks (95% accuracy validated in LAB-003)
Visibility at Scale: Track agent actions, memory growth, and drift in real-time (EXECUTION_LEDGER from LAB-007)
competitiveMoat: LAB-validated multi-agent patterns (3+ experiments with L2 evidence)

competitiveIntelligence.marketGapIdentified: The competitive landscape has a SMB coordination gap: Large enterprises use custom integrations + enterprise platforms, Dev shops use coding agents (Cursor, Replit), SMBs (retail, consulting, marketing) have no multi-agent orchestration solution with persistent memory.
threatSignalsToMonitor: Anthropic expands beyond devs (High Priority) - Direct competitive threat
OpenAI adds persistent memory (High Priority) - Neutralizes key differentiation
Cursor/Replit add multi-agent (Medium Priority) - Erodes dev segment
New entrant in SMB coordination (Medium Priority) - Direct competitive threat in target ICP
